summary: EXP-S (#2, 0.8.14) coexisting-index / multi-index-write substrate must not foreclose the OPP-12 projection registry that will later sit on it — design the multi-index write so a declarative filterable/rankable/searchable registry can wrap it without a substrate reshape
body: Grounded caveat for the 0.8.14 EXP-S build (Slice-0 ADR + Slice-5 keystone). OPP-12's engine-owned projection registry (filterable/rankable/searchable declarations; same-txn FTS/filter, async vector via the existing worker + the atomic dense_readiness flip) is architecturally a DECLARATIVE FRONT-END over exactly the coexisting-index / incremental multi-index write that EXP-S builds -- EXP-S is the substrate the projection registry will sit on (OPP-12 lands >=0.9.x). No dependency edge forces ordering, and EXP-S touches NONE of OPP-12's surface (verified: plan-0.8.14 has no SearchHit / lifecycle / projection scope). But the Slice-0 substrate ADR should design the multi-index write so a later projection registry can WRAP it without a substrate reshape (don't foreclose per-kind declarative projection specs or the async-vector dense_readiness flip). Re-check at OPP-12 scheduling. See [[opp12-record-lifecycle-protocol]].

summary: typescript-6 major looks PARTIALLY APPLIED: src/ts/package.json already pins typescript ^6.0.3 (matches its lockfile) while Dependabot PR #67 (typescript 6.0.3) is still OPEN — the typescript LBO should reconcile (verify + close #67, or complete the bump), not assume it's unstarted
body: Surfaced during Library Sweep #2 (typescript is a MAJOR, out of the 6-minor scope, left untouched). State is ambiguous: the src/ts manifest+lockfile are internally consistent at typescript ^6.0.3, yet PR #67 was never merged/closed. So the typescript-6 upgrade is either already effectively in place (PR stale, just needs closing) or half-applied. The typescript LBO (or the 0.8.20 major sweep) must verify the actual tsc build under ^6.0.3 and reconcile the open PR rather than treat typescript-6 as greenfield. Ref src/ts/package.json + PR #67.

summary: DESIGN-ADDRESSED: the 0.8.14 Slice-0 ADR (dff4830c, D5) DISCHARGES this caveat — the D2 per-kind index-target set is the seam a later OPP-12 projection registry wraps; FTS/filter same-txn + vector async-via-worker preserved; terminal-cursor readiness kept per-kind-extensible. Now WATCHING: re-check the Slice-5 keystone actually honors the seam, and again at OPP-12 scheduling
body: Advanced from open->watching. The ratified Slice-0 substrate ADR explicitly discharges TC-1 in §D5 (verified from git dff4830c): the engine already owns the async substrate a future OPP-12 projection registry needs (projection workers + _fathomdb_projection_terminal readiness = durable dense_readiness equivalent; per-kind kind_is_vector_indexed), and the ADR obligates EXP-S to (a) keep the D2 per-kind index-target set a wrappable seam, (b) preserve FTS/filter same-txn + vector async-via-worker (no collapse to sync), (c) keep terminal-cursor readiness per-kind-extensible. Trigger to re-check: the Slice-5 keystone implementation honors these; then close/re-open at OPP-12 scheduling (>=0.9.x).

summary: Engine::bm25f_search scores by re-tokenizing each field via a temp-FTS5 round-trip (fts5_tokenize) per field/candidate — correctness-first, but there is NO perf gate on bm25f_search. A future perf pass / Slice 40 / the 1-2M scaling work should weigh this before F5 rides a hot path
body: Surfaced at 0.8.14 Slice 10 (F5 BM25F, c57e4e99); flagged by BOTH codex §9 and the implementer. The in-engine BM25F scorer (justified ADR-0.8.1 deviation — FTS5 bm25() pins k1/b, so scoring is done in-engine to honor R-F5-1's tunable b) computes per-field term stats by re-tokenizing each field through fts5_tokenize per field per candidate. Correct + tokenization-faithful (the fix-1 that resolved codex's medium finding), but O(fields x candidates) tokenizer round-trips with no perf gate on the path. Engine-internal / experimental today, so NOT blocking. Re-weigh before F5 is put on a hot/large query path: a perf gate at Slice 40 (release readiness) or during the 1-2M scaling work, or a caching/precompute optimization. Ref c57e4e99 (bm25f_search).

summary: ENFORCE the HITL mandate in tooling: the eu7/eval harness + eval runners MUST DEFAULT to GPU embedding (embed-cuda + FATHOMDB_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda:0, rerank-cuda for rerank) when the 3090s have compute+memory room, CPU fallback only -- so no agent has to remember the flags. Exclude the K620 (FATHOMDB_GPU_EXCLUDE); check nvidia-smi room before dispatch; verify GPU engaged
body: Standing HITL mandate 2026-07-05 (policy: dev/design/gpu-eval-activities-policy.md; memory repo-must-use-gpu-for-eval-when-3090-room). Root cause: a fresh 0.8.14 eu7 run crawled on the CPU embedder (~0.43 docs/sec, seed-timeout-prone) with both 3090s at 0% idle. GPU is 15-93x faster (0.8.7) and CPU<->CUDA is 1-bit identical (fidelity-safe). Per fix-the-tooling-not-people: make GPU-when-room the DEFAULT in the eu7 harness (eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs currently builds --features default-embedder = CPU) + the eval runners (agent-build.sh / eval scripts), with a room-check (nvidia-smi util+free-mem) and CPU fallback only when the 3090s are busy/full. Watch the GPU-eval build traps (libcuda bundling / stale .so shadowing, eval-environment-operational-notes). SCOPE: internal eval/dev/CI only; shipped-library default (CPU/opt-in) unchanged. PLACEMENT TBD (HITL): fold into 0.8.14 Slice 40, or a fast-follow pico. Interim: pass the env/flags explicitly per run (the current eu7 re-run already switched to GPU).

summary: REFINED (HITL 2025-07-05): CPU is NOT a general fallback. Full eval/embed/rerank suites ALWAYS run on GPU; CPU is permitted ONLY for two narrow probes -- (1) simple CPU-compatibility testing, (2) CPU-library repeatability/compatibility checks. 'Feature compatibility' = check compat with CPU libraries, NOT run full suites on both CPU+GPU. When 3090s busy: wait/queue or use the other 3090, never a full CPU run. Harness/eval-runner default = GPU for full runs
body: Tightens the enforcement scope. The eu7/eval harness + eval runners MUST default to GPU for full runs; there is NO CPU full-suite fallback. CPU is reserved for minimal, targeted compatibility probes only: a CPU-code-path build/run smoke, and a CPU-library repeatability/compatibility check -- never a mirror of the full GPU suite. On a busy GPU, wait/queue or use cuda:1, don't run the full suite on CPU. Policy: dev/design/gpu-eval-activities-policy.md (updated). Placement of the tooling change still TBD (HITL): 0.8.14 Slice 40 or a fast-follow pico.

summary: Re-baseline the eu7 0.90 recall floor for the GROWN 18,472-doc cross-source corpus. The floor + the 0.896 CPU baseline (ci_hi 0.925) predate the musique (Jun 16) + compmix (Jul 3) cross-source additions, which mechanically lower recall@10 at fixed N. Needs a fresh CPU same-backend eu7 N=7667 measurement on the current corpus + an HITL threshold ruling on a corpus-appropriate floor. Separate from 0.8.14 (D6 zero vec0 rewrite excludes a regression)
body: Surfaced at 0.8.14 Slice 20: a GPU eu7 run read vector-stage recall@10 N=7667=0.833 (ci_hi 0.864 < 0.90) vs the historical CPU baseline 0.896 (ci_hi 0.925 PASS); N=1000=0.950 PASS. Two verified confounds: (1) cross-backend (GPU embed vs CPU baseline; near-threshold 1-bit-quant flips at large N) -- fixed forward: eu7/fidelity gates now run CPU same-backend per gpu-eval-activities-policy.md; (2) corpus growth (musique+compmix added post-baseline). D6 (codex-verified zero vec0 rewrite @ Slice 5) excludes a 0.8.14 regression, so Slice 20 closed on the D6 no-op basis (HITL). OPEN: is 0.90 still right for the grown corpus? Resolve via a fresh CPU same-backend eu7 N=7667 on the current corpus, then an HITL floor ruling (keep 0.90 or re-baseline). Placement TBD.

summary: embed_batch_cls is py-only (no TS binding) — a py-first X1 asymmetry surfaced+deferred at 0.8.14 Slice 40. A future binding-parity release should DECIDE: add the TS embed_batch_cls, or ratify the py-first asymmetry as intended. Currently guarded by the new drift-guard test (src/python/tests/test_surface.py, a8e28ff2) + board R-X-1 so it can't silently regress
body: At 0.8.14 Slice 40 X1, the embed_batch_cls helper (CLS-pooled GPU batch embed, shipped in Slice 25 for eval) is exposed py-only with a .pyi stub; there is no TS binding. Previously a silent harness blind spot; now non-vacuously ASSERTED as a known py-only asymmetry (test a8e28ff2). This is not a 0.8.14 gap (embed_batch_cls is an eval/GPU helper, not a governed verb) but a binding-parity decision for a future release: (a) add the TS embed_batch_cls for full X1 symmetry, or (b) ratify it as a deliberate py-first asymmetry (eval-only helper). Distinct from TC-5 (eu7 floor). Placement TBD (a future SDK-parity or 0.8.16+ release).

summary: C-1 co-design TIMING (EntityTypeSpec -> ProjectionSpec): Memex's Commission C / entity-schema-registry is its ACTUAL 0.5.x work and is LIVE NOW; it builds toward the C-1 seam. C-1 is HARD (one declarative registration flow -- app never syncs two registries). FathomDB's projection registry is 0.9.1, but the EntityTypeSpec->ProjectionSpec co-design ADR should be AGREED before Memex locks Commission C's registry shape, or we recreate the two-registry-sync drift C-1 exists to prevent. Near-term cross-repo coordination watch (raise to HITL) even though the FathomDB build is >=0.9.x.

Cause-A TYPED SearchHit.id round-trip (OPP-12 C-2 / F-8a): typed IdSpace {space,value} + write_cursor->logical_id swap. VERIFIED NOT started on main -- SearchHit.id is u64 (lib.rs:1109), stable_id is additive Option<String> (1132), no IdSpace type. Breaking => >=0.9.x (0.9.0 P1-S-C in the draft). This is MEMEX's #1 P0 need: gates the whole OPP-12 Memex-side pair, graph-arm hit->goal/WMAction reconnection (OPP-1 graph path), and typed hit-level data-fitness beyond the string stable_id base. Standing cross-repo blocker; lands-together with the C-2 contract + anonymous-node surrogate minting.

TC-7 ADVANCED open->in_progress: C-1 co-design two-shot loop KICKED OFF. Fresh sub-ledger dev/design/record-lifecycle-protocol/OPP-12-sub-ledger.jsonl created (protocol + SHOT-1 FATHOM posted; full text OPP-12-C1-shot1.md). SHOT 1 forces 6 bounded questions; the load-bearing near-term catch = Q1 (add optional AttributeSpec role hint) + Q5 (engine-as-derived-cache) must be agreed BEFORE Memex Commission C locks AttributeSpec (breaking re-serialization otherwise). Awaiting HITL review of SHOT-1 content before Memex is routed to answer (SHOT 2).

TC-7 ADVANCED in_progress->converged-pending-hitl: OPP-12 C-1 two-shot co-design CONVERGED in 2 rounds. Memex agreed Q1(field landed @95ed450)/Q3/Q5; Q2/Q4/Q6 accepted with FathomDB refinements (Q4 engine-needs-no-provisional-concept; Q2 explicit no-migration; over-build restated). Converged contract OPP-12-C1-converged-contract.md (seed for joint 0.9.1 P2.S0 ADR). Deferred to P2.S0: apply-atomicity + tokenizer/embedder defaults. Co-lands 0.9.1 / Memex 0.5.x-succ; build!=adopt. NEXT: HITL ratification of the converged C-1 contract.

TC-7 RESOLVED (converged-pending-hitl -> ratified): HITL ratified the C-1 converged contract 2026-07-07. C-1 co-design is closed + agreed (OPP-12-C1-converged-contract.md); the near-term timing risk is retired (break-if-late AttributeSpec.index field frozen inert in Memex Commission C @95ed450). Remaining C-1 work is the 0.9.1 P2.S-F engine build (a future-release item, separately build-authorized at 0.9.x §7.8), NOT a standing todo watch. Closing TC-7.

TC-8 PLACEMENT reconciled to plans (HITL 'ledgers secondary -> reflect new release planning'): the OPP-12 C-2 typed SearchHit.id swap (write_cursor->logical_id + typed IdSpace) is NO LONGER >=0.9.x. Per master F-19/F-20 it lands IN the 0.8.x line at 0.8.19 (odd, label-only) OPP-12 Phase-1 [existence axis . transition/purge . C-2 swap . schema mig . X1]. Still VERIFIED-NOT-STARTED on main (SearchHit.id u64, stable_id additive). BUILD-AUTHORIZATION for 0.8.19/0.8.20 remains a SEPARATE pending HITL gate (build != adopt); this entry reconciles PLACEMENT only. Memex's #1 P0 need.

TC-7 SLOT CORRECTION (stays RESOLVED): the C-1 EntityTypeSpec->ProjectionSpec co-land is NO LONGER '0.9.1 P2.S-F'. Per master F-19/F-20 (and OPP-12 sub-ledger seq 8) C-1 co-lands at FathomDB 0.8.20 (OPP-12 Phase-2 + breaking-pair publish), DESIGN UNCHANGED. Read every '0.9.1'/'P2.S-F' in seq 13 + the converged contract as 0.8.20. No re-ratification (scheduling change, not contract change).

TC-8 BUILD-AUTHORIZED (HITL 2026-07-08, master F-21): the OPP-12 C-2 typed SearchHit.id swap (write_cursor->logical_id + typed IdSpace) build gate is CLEARED at its 0.8.19 Phase-1 slot. Build != adopt (adoption arms still per-item gated); publish stays a separate per-x.y.z HITL call. Stays OPEN as a tracked build item (VERIFIED-NOT-STARTED on main); state advances open->build-authorized. In-sequence after 0.8.18 (not reordered); Steward flagged a concurrent-schema-migration hazard vs parallelizing into 0.8.16. Ladder plan-0.8.19.md still holds pre-F-19 free-threading content -> OPP-12 Phase-1 ladder TBA when the slot approaches.

TC-9 (NEW): bump ort =2.0.0-rc.10 -> 2.0 STABLE when it releases. HITL accepted the RC pin (2026-07-08) for 0.8.16 ONNX behind the optional non-default onnx-embedder feature (EVAL-only; default build/wheels pull zero new deps). rc.12 currently fails to build (VitisAI EP mismatch); rc.10 is newest-that-builds; 1.x is EOL. Watch for ort 2.0 stable and bump the pin (Library-Bump-Steward candidate). Low urgency (opt-in EVAL feature).

TC-9 EXTENSION (D3/L3 finding, 2026-07-09, Steward-verified): ONNX-GPU-EP cross-backend Delta measured OOB (durable doc git:76568975). candle-CPU(Cls) <-> ONNX-CUDA-EP(Cls; ORT 1.26.0 GPU + cuDNN 9.5 + RTX3090 cuda:1) = 2/17280 raw sign flips (1/17280 proxy-mean-centered), cosine 0.99999996, L2 <= 5.4e-4, deterministic x3, EFFECTIVE PROVIDER VERIFIED CUDA -- the FIRST non-byte-identical cross-backend leg (both prior CPU legs = 0 flips; pure GPU FP divergence, not a broken export). FINDING: as-shipped OrtBgeEmbedder under ort=2.0.0-rc.10 (default-features=false) CANNOT instantiate the CUDA EP -- ort::init() is never called (Steward-verified ABSENT in ort_bge.rs; the EP-dispatch + loud-fallback code IS present) -> ORT 'DefaultLogger not registered' -> LOUD CPU fallback. Proven binding-not-host: same native stack runs CUDA via Python + via a standalone rc.10 probe WITH a one-time ort::init().commit(). NOT a shipped hazard (ONNX = opt-in non-default EVAL feature; fallback is LOUD not silent; default library path = candle-CPU, unaffected). ACTION folded into TC-9 (ort 2.0-stable bump): (a) add a one-time ORT env init so ONNX GPU/cross-vendor EPs are reachable, (b) re-verify CUDA-EP session creation on 2.0-stable. #5/D4 BEARING: none now -- the D4 same-identity floor stays tight (0 flips; same-identity candle CPU<->CUDA was 0/6144 @0.8.7), and the 2-flip GPU-EP leg is a DISTINCT '-onnx' identity already refused by identity + R-VEQ-5 additive-only; a FUTURE cross-vendor portability ADR must budget a NON-ZERO GPU-EP flip rate.

TC-10 (NEW, HITL-directed 2026-07-09): #5 vector-equivalence probe OPEN-LATENCY. The #5 keystone re-embeds the 45-probe set at EVERY open of a vector-kind DB (the cross-open backend-drift check) -> a new OPEN-time overhead. NOT a query-latency / AC-012/013/020 breach (the read path adds only an open-time AtomicBool dense_disabled check; representation unchanged). ACTION: (a) MEASURE the open-latency overhead (45 CPU embeds at open); (b) consider IDENTITY-GATED probe caching -- re-run the probe only when the embedder identity/revision CHANGES since last open, not every open (the EmbedderIdentity pre-filter already tracks identity) -- as a future-micro optimization; (c) optionally a GA open-latency AC/measured note. Surfaced at 0.8.18 Slice-40 GA verification (orchestrator); HITL directed TC-track, NOT a GA blocker (0.8.18 closed label-only). Owner: future micro (convenient slot >=0.8.21 or fold into an OPP-12 slice). Correctness-first: the every-open probe is correct; caching is an optimization.

OPEN-TC SCHEDULE RATIFIED (HITL 2026-07-09; master F-22). Slot per open item: TC-8 (OPP-12 C-2 SearchHit.id swap write_cursor->logical_id+IdSpace) = 0.8.19 Phase-1 (it IS the work; build-auth F-21). embed_batch_cls TS-binding parity (py-only X1 asymmetry, seq8) = 0.8.19/0.8.20 (decide add-TS vs ratify-py-first, folded into their X1 parity). eu7 BASIS decision (no-op vs real) = 0.8.20 (real-publish gate; bounded re-baseline if OPP-12 touches retrieval/gold-keying). TC-5 (full eu7 0.90 floor re-baseline, grown 18,472-doc corpus, seq7) = 0.8.23 scale-bound soft (natural home). TC-10 (#5 open-latency, seq19) = MEASURE at 0.8.19, optimize (identity-gated probe caching) >=0.8.21 only if warranted. bm25f_search perf gate (seq4) = 0.8.21 (#13 bench harness). TC-9 (ort 2.0-stable bump + ort::init GPU-EP fix, seq17/18) = 0.8.22 dep-migrations or opportunistic pico when ort 2.0-stable ships. typescript-6/Dependabot #67 (seq2) = next Library Sweep pico / 0.8.22. GPU-eval-mandate tooling enforcement (seq5/6) = opportunistic tooling task, pair with 0.8.23/24 heavy eval. RESOLVED items (EXP-S/projection caveat seq1/3, TC-7 C-1 seq9-13) need no slot.

TC-11 (NEW, HITL 2026-07-09; master F-23 GUARDRAIL): 0.8.20 (OPP-12 Phase-2) MUST pin the doc-seeded 'h:' end-state BEFORE Phase-2 proceeds -- is doc-seeded/anonymous SearchHit.id 'h:<content-hash>' TERMINAL-FOREVER, FORWARD-MINT-ONLY (new anonymous nodes get l: surrogates, old stay h:), or BACKFILLED (h:->l: remap of the existing ~18k doc-seeded rows + real-gold remap)? The genuine long-run risk (surfaced by the 0.8.19 Slice-0 adversarial audit) is a PERMANENT SPLIT: pre-0.8.20 doc-seeded h: vs post-0.8.20 anonymous l: for identical re-ingested content. This exists INDEPENDENT of 1a's timing (1a correctly deferred the decision to where the natural-key/projection registry lives). LEADING HYPOTHESIS (audit inference, confirm at Phase-2 design): 'h:' is TERMINAL -- doc-chunks are NOT EntityTypeSpec entities, so the C-1 anonymous-entity surrogate path may not apply to them -> h: terminal -> 1a fully safe, no backfill ever. ACTION: de-stale plan-0.8.20.md (currently holds pre-F-19 dep-migrations content) + author OPP-12 Phase-2 ladder including this end-state decision record. Owner: 0.8.20 Slice-0.

TC-8 (OPP-12 C-2 SearchHit.id swap: write_cursor+stable_id -> typed IdSpace) RESOLVED — shipped in 0.8.19 Phase-1 (label-only, origin/main 578fe20b; master F-24; OPP-12 sub-ledger seq 9). u64->IdSpace {space,value}; to_prefixed() == prior stable_id byte-for-byte => real-gold keying a true no-op (no gold-remap); breaking surface built label-only, PUBLISHES at 0.8.20. TC-11 (0.8.20 doc-seeded h: end-state pin + de-stale plan-0.8.20) REMAINS OPEN and is the FIRST Phase-2 step. NEW carries for the 0.8.20 commission, from the rubric-eval v3 pilot findings on 0.8.19 (HARD GATE PASS 12/12, 84.4%): (a) G6 — run Py/TS parity BEFORE land as one unit on any surface-touching slice (0.8.19 ran X1 green AFTER land due to a native-import env trap; treat an env trap as a landing blocker, not a follow-up); (b) TC-RUBRIC-7 — persist every codex §9 review transcript to a durable release-namespaced path (e.g. scratchpad/codex/0.8.20/slice-N-review.txt) so B1/B2 independent re-verification is possible after the fact; (c) TC-RUBRIC-5 / A6 — dedicated checkout/worktree per orchestration (the shared-checkout guard; see the steward guardrail-fix proposal). Fold (a)+(b) into the 0.8.20 X0 process gate.

0.8.20 Slice-0 ERASURE PRIMITIVE inputs (HITL steer 2026-07-11, reframes the GDPR/purge question). KEY REFRAME: the erasure axis is PROVENANCE (source_id / excise_source), NOT the l:/h: id-space -> pin A is ORTHOGONAL to erasure and survives regardless. Slice-0 must ask 'is excise_source a COMPLETE erasure primitive, and is provenance MANDATORY?' with THREE concrete work-items (defects, not design debates): (1) ONE shared row-owned delete-set consumed by BOTH purge_inner and excise_source_inner -- the bug is two erasure verbs maintaining the target-table list INDEPENDENTLY, so the next projection table reopens the hole (per memory: excise_source misses the content-storing search_index_v2 => body survives erasure); the RED test asserts on RAW TABLE CONTENTS, not search results (leak is data-at-rest, never surfaces in results). (2) Fix ingest provenance -- use the caller's d.source_doc_id, NOT the model's echo (straight bug fix). (3) source_id MANDATORY (String, not Option) on PreparedWrite::Node/Edge -- this is where the breaking-changes lever pays, converting erase-by-provenance from best-effort to TOTAL; PLUS expose excise_source in the napi/py SDKs (else Memex has NO erasure capability at all). THE ONE HITL DECISION (not a fix): pre-change NULL-provenance rows get a BACKFILL or a documented DATED BOUNDARY. See memory [[erasure-axis-is-provenance-excise-source-gap]].

TC-11 CLOSED + seq-23 CORRECTION (HITL 2026-07-12; master F-25). (1) TC-11 RATIFIED = pin A, TERMINAL-FOREVER: anonymous/doc-seeded nodes stay h:<content-hash> PERMANENTLY; structural-lifecycle-contract.md 2(ii) is OVERRULED; the anonymous-surrogate leg is CANCELLED, not deferred (a surrogate serves ONLY registry-admitted governed entities, decided at write time; a stored row's id-space is never re-derived). Accepted corollary: an anonymous row + a later governed row for the same thing BOTH stay active and BOTH surface in search (NULL never collides with the partial-unique index) -- the engine does not dedupe them. (2) CORRECTION TO seq 23 -- its work-item (3) said 'source_id mandatory + excise_source IN THE SDKs'. The excise_source-in-SDK half is WRONG and is RETRACTED: excise_source is the RECOVERY seam (REQ-026, built to excise a corrupt ingest) and is named in REQ-037 as SDK-forbidden. But 'CLI-only' is ALSO wrong as a resolution: NO CLI SHIPS to SDK consumers (the wheel declares no [project.scripts]; npm no 'bin'), so erasure is UNREACHABLE. Real policy = 'RECOVERY-NAMED verbs are CLI-only', NOT 'destructive => CLI-only' -- proof: purge(logical_id) is ALREADY an SDK verb (py:1297, napi:1102) despite being named in REQ-037, because AC-041 tests only the REQ-054 five-name denylist. RULING: excise_source stays CLI-only; ADD erase_source(source_id) as a first-class SDK LIFECYCLE verb (not a recovery name => AC-041 stays GREEN, denylist stays five), one shared engine path. REQ-037 amended with a lawful-erasure carve-out; purge_logical_id struck from its forbidden list (shipped code already contradicted it). (3) ERASURE DEFECT CLASS WIDENED: the row-owned projection table list is implicit and duplicated FIVE times, not two. search_index_v2 (content-storing FTS5, holds the body verbatim) is written by project_canonical_node_row:11806 and deleted ONLY by purge:6229 -- excise_source_inner:6398, rebuild_shadow_state:6515, and reproject_search_index_after_tokenizer_upgrade:9515 NEVER touch it => erased bodies survive on disk; the table accumulates every body ever written; and v1/v2 diverge after a tokenizer upgrade. Also: NO edge projector exists (edge projection is inlined in commit_batch) so projector-replay rebuild must cover edges; truncate_wal() EXISTS (:6379, typed Busy) but purge/excise never call it; telemetry retains l: ids post-erasure (:4462); op-store has no record-level delete (:10083 is a cap sweep). RED tests MUST assert on RAW TABLE CONTENTS -- both FTS read paths gate on canonical_nodes, so a test that SEARCHES for the excised text PASSES on the broken code. (4) plan-0.8.20.md DE-STALED + AUTHORED (dep-migration runbook removed -> owed to plan-0.8.22.md). Design of record: dev/design/0.8.20-erasure-and-h-end-state-v4.md (codex 9 rounds 1-3).

eu7 is a CPU SAME-BACKEND gate: a GPU-run eu7 number is NOT comparable to the 0.90 floor and manufactures a FALSE REGRESSION. Verified at 0.8.20 Slice-0 from run artifacts: CPU (GA-signoff-eu7-remeasure-20260608T172804Z.json) n=7667 vector-stage recall@10 = 0.8960 CI [0.8640, 0.9250] => PASS on the one-sided gate (ci_hi >= 0.90). GPU (dev/plans/runs/0.8.14-slice-20-eu7-gpu-run-20260705T205222Z.log:73) n=7667 = 0.8330 CI [0.8010, 0.8640] => FAIL. The delta is a cross-backend quantization artifact, NOT a code regression (it is the TC-5 floor re-baseline driver, scheduled 0.8.23). CONSEQUENCE: the standing HITL GPU-eval mandate (repo-must-use-gpu-for-eval-when-3090-room) DOES NOT APPLY to the eu7 recall gate -- by that mandate's own 'fidelity-safe' caveat. Any release taking an eu7 basis decision (0.8.20 R-20-EU7 at Slice 40; TC-5 at 0.8.23) MUST capture and compare on CPU only, and must compare against a CPU reference. Running eu7 on the 3090s to 'honor the mandate' would wrongly force a re-baseline.

eu7 harness has TWO vacuous-green hazards; any release running eu7 must guard both. (1) THE DOCUMENTED RUN COMMAND DOES NOT WORK. src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs:85-86 documents 'AGENT_LONG=1 cargo test -p fathomdb-engine --features default-embedder --test eu7_real_corpus_ac'. That fails with EXIT 101: 'target eu7_real_corpus_ac in package fathomdb-engine requires the features: operator'. Working invocation: --features default-embedder,operator. Fix the docstring (0.8.20 Slice 5 owns it). (2) THE CORPUS IS UNREACHABLE FROM A LINKED WORKTREE. data/corpus-data/ is gitignored (.gitignore:9) and exists ONLY in the primary checkout (2.1 GB). From a worktree, load_subset_or_skip finds nothing, the test prints [skip] and returns EXIT 0 -- a full vacuous green having measured nothing. Workaround used at 0.8.20 Slice-0: symlink data/corpus-data from the primary checkout into the worktree and exclude it via .git/info/exclude (no tracked file changes). GUARD REQUIRED: assert a NON-SKIP WITNESS (e.g. the EU7_SETUP real_docs=N line) rather than trusting exit 0. Compounded by the known trap background-exit-masks-real-exit: a trailing echo around the cargo invocation reported exit 0 while the real exit was 101 -- this recurred live during Slice-0.

AC ID-SPACE ABOVE AC-076 IS RESERVED -- do not mint new ACs by taking 'max AC id + 1'. Verified at 0.8.20 Slice-0: the highest DEFINED, NON-RESERVED acceptance criterion is AC-076 (dev/acceptance.md:1147). AC-077 (:1286) is a RESERVED PLACEHOLDER, explicitly 'not yet a gate; no fabricated numbers', held for the IR-eval IR-1/IR-2 initiative (agentic IR / evidence-recall, maps to REQ-067, all thresholds TBD). AC-078 is CONDITIONALLY reserved to the SAME initiative -- :1297 reads '+ AC-078... only if the consensus splits the measure'. TRAP: a naive AC-036

AC-078 returns AC-078 from that PROSE and will mislead an agent into minting a colliding id. plan-0.8.20.md section 3 states 'AC ceiling today = AC-077' and that new ACs 'continue from' it -- following that literally collides with a live reservation. 0.8.20 Slice-0 RECOMMENDS minting from AC-079 (HITL decision owed). Any future release minting ACs must read the reservation note at dev/acceptance.md:1286-1300 first, and per the locked-acceptance.md policy should prefer tracking by requirement id + TDD test name over minting per-feature ACs at all.

THE ERASURE AUDIT TRAIL IS DESTRUCTIBLE -- refutes the v4 design of record. dev/design/0.8.20-erasure-and-h-end-state-v4.md section 3.6 asserts excise_source_audit 'retains source_id PERMANENTLY (:6479) -- by design; that is the audit trail', and plan-0.8.20.md section 8 repeats it as the justification for the non-PII source_id rule. VERIFIED FALSE at 4ca70ba6: the audit row is written into operational_mutations (engine lib.rs:6479), and enforce_provenance_retention (:10070) sweeps THAT SAME TABLE cap-first, oldest-id-first, with NO COLLECTION FILTER (:10083-10089: DELETE FROM operational_mutations WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM operational_mutations ORDER BY id LIMIT ?1)). Erasure audit rows are swept like any other op-store row. TWO CONSEQUENCES: (1) ACCOUNTABILITY GAP -- demonstrating THAT an erasure occurred is a distinct obligation from performing it; a retention sweep can silently destroy the proof. (2) COLLISION WITH WORK ITEM 9 -- excise_collection_record operates on the op-store, the SAME table the audit rows live in, so the erasure record and the payloads being erased share one retention pool. The non-PII source_id rule STILL STANDS (an unswept audit row may still hold it) but its stated rationale is argued from a false premise and must be corrected. RECOMMENDED (HITL, decision owed): exempt the erasure-audit collection from enforce_provenance_retention by filtering the sweep by collection, and state the audit-durability guarantee explicitly. This is a RETENTION-POLICY change, not a pure defect repair, hence HITL-gated.

THE PUBLISH DRY-RUN GUARD IS DEAD AND HAS BEEN RED SINCE 0.8.14 -- fix before the first real publish. scripts/tests/test_actionlint_fixture.sh:53-56 asserts that every publish-rust-<tier> job block in .github/workflows/release.yml contains the literal 'cargo publish --dry-run -p '. It FAILS on the FIRST tier (t1-embedder-api), which makes ./scripts/agent-test.sh exit 1 wholesale. dev/plans/runs/0.8.14-slice-40-output.json:23 already recorded this as 'the only remaining agent-verify red' -- it has now been carried unfixed across 0.8.14/0.8.16/0.8.18/0.8.19 into 0.8.20. DIAGNOSIS (verified at 4ca70ba6): the WORKFLOW IS FINE, THE TEST IS STALE. release.yml:248 no longer inlines the cargo command; it delegates to 'bash scripts/release/cargo-publish-if-new.sh --dry-run fathomdb-embedder-api', and that helper does forward to 'cargo publish --dry-run -p <crate>' (cargo-publish-if-new.sh:14). So publish BEHAVIOR is intact and this is not a publish defect. THE REAL HARM IS THE MASKED GATE: a permanently-red assertion is a gate nobody reads, so any GENUINE publish-wiring regression would now be invisible -- and 0.8.20 is the FIRST REAL PUBLISH (manifests 0.8.9 -> 0.8.20, cargo order embedder -> engine, and a pushed v* tag auto-fires REAL crates/PyPI/npm). Same class as perf-recall-gates-masked. FIX (Slice 40, R-20-PUB): re-point the assertion at the delegation -- assert the job invokes cargo-publish-if-new.sh with --dry-run AND that the helper forwards to cargo publish --dry-run -p -- so the guard is live again, then confirm ./scripts/agent-test.sh is GREEN before the publish gate.

SLICE 5's SourceId NEWTYPE WILL BREAK THE eu7 HARNESS (and every other in-tree PreparedWrite construction) -- budget for it. R-20-E3 / v4 section 3.2 replaces 'source_id: Option<String>' with a mandatory SourceId newtype on the public PreparedWrite, making 'no provenance' INEXPRESSIBLE. That is the point of the change -- but it means EVERY existing in-tree construction of PreparedWrite with 'source_id: None' stops compiling. CONFIRMED INSTANCE: src/rust/crates/fathomdb-engine/tests/eu7_real_corpus_ac.rs:405 seeds the real-corpus haystack with 'source_id: None, logical_id: None'. eu7 is the ANN-fidelity gate feeding R-20-EU7 at Slice 40, so if Slice 5 lands the newtype without updating this harness, the Slice-40 eu7 basis decision cannot even be measured -- the test will not build. v4 section 3.2 only anticipated 'two internal callers (:5186, :5209, the doc(hidden) poison fixture)'; the TEST-side callers were not enumerated. ACTION for Slice 5: sweep ALL constructions of PreparedWrite across src/ AND tests/ (not just the two v4 names), give each a real SourceId, and re-verify the eu7 harness still BUILDS before landing. Cheap to do with the change, expensive to discover at Slice 40.

PROCESS: committing a codex transcript into the reviewed range pollutes the NEXT review's diff. TC-RUBRIC-7 requires persisting every codex section-9 transcript to a durable release-namespaced path. At 0.8.20 Slice-0 the transcript was committed to dev/plans/runs/codex/0.8.20/ and the fix-1 RE-review was then run with the same '--base 4ca70ba6'. Because the base was unchanged, the re-review's diff INCLUDED the previously-committed transcript file, so codex re-read its own prior findings as though they were part of the change under review -- the old [P2] finding text reappeared verbatim in the new transcript, which is easy to misread as 'the finding was not fixed'. It was fixed (verified independently: pre-fix exit 128, post-fix exit 0 under codex's own reproduction), and codex's actual terminal verdict was PASS. MITIGATIONS (pick one, for every later slice): (a) re-review against a base that already contains the prior transcript, (b) persist transcripts on a path excluded from the review scope, or (c) commit transcripts only AFTER the slice's final review round. Recommend (c) as the simplest: it keeps the reviewed diff to real code+docs and still satisfies TC-RUBRIC-7 at slice close. Also note the transcript path was pinned as .log (not .md) to match the existing 0.8.16-slice-*-codex-review-*.log convention and stay out of markdownlint scope.
